title: Brand Context Protocol — voice.md
description: Schema for BCP v0.2 voice.md daughter files. v0.2 adds essence, traits, vocabulary.substitutions, forbidden_topics, naming, messaging_tiers, topic_templates, and audience_modulation to the v0.1 field set. All new fields are optional. A v0.1 voice.md with no new fields is a valid v0.2 document.

bcp_version.description: Version of the BCP specification this file conforms to.

parent.description: Path to the root brand.md file, typically /.well-known/brand.md.

last_updated.description: ISO 8601 date of the last meaningful change to this file.

essence.description: One-liner positioning statements. Each key is optional. Values must be single sentences.

vision.description: The future the brand is working toward.

purpose.description: The benefit the brand creates for people.

brand_promise.description: The commitment the brand makes to its customers.

traits.description: Bipartite voice trait list defining what the voice is and what it refuses to be.

is.description: The positive trait. Agents should apply this as a style target.

not.description: The negative trait. Agents should treat this as a disqualifier.

description.description: Optional authoring guidance explaining the distinction.

vocabulary.description: Preferred and avoided terms, plus explicit substitution pairs.

prefer.description: Terms agents must use. Each entry carries a word and optional usage note.

use.description: When and how to use this term.

substitutions.description: Explicit preferred-term to avoided-term mappings with optional example sentences. New in v0.2.

avoid.description: The term agents must not use.

forbidden_topics.description: Topics the brand does not associate with. New in v0.2.

topic.description: The subject or category to avoid.

severity.description: 'never' means agents MUST NOT associate the brand with this topic. 'avoid' means agents SHOULD NOT associate the brand with this topic absent explicit instruction.

naming.description: How the brand name is written. Consumers must use canonical and must not use any forbidden_variant. New in v0.2.

canonical.description: The correct form of the brand name. Consumers must use this form.

forbidden_variants.description: Incorrectly formed name variants. Consumers must not use these.

sub_brands.description: Sub-brand names with optional usage notes.

acronym_rules.description: Rules for how abbreviations and acronyms are handled.

messaging_tiers.description: The same message at multiple lengths. Agents select the tier appropriate to the surface. New in v0.2.

topic.description: The subject this tier set covers.

blunt.description: The shortest possible version. Use for character-constrained surfaces.

topic_templates.description: How the brand talks about specific recurring subjects. New in v0.2.

example_good.description: An example of on-brand copy for this topic.

example_bad.description: An example of off-brand copy for this topic.

audience_modulation.description: How voice shifts per audience segment. A lightweight alternative to full audiences/{segment}.md files. New in v0.2.

audience.description: Audience identifier. Should be one of: prospect, customer, lapsed_customer, developer, employee, investor, regulator, press. Custom values are permitted.

tone_shift.description: How tone changes for this audience.

trait_overrides.description: Trait overrides that replace or extend the root traits block for this audience.

rules.description: Sentence-level writing rules. Each rule is a single imperative sentence.

channels.description: Do/don't examples per surface. Keys are channel names. Values carry surfaces and example copy.

surfaces.description: The surfaces or contexts this channel entry covers.

shows_up_in.description: Human-readable description of where this channel appears.

good.description: Example of on-brand copy for this channel.

bad.description: Example of off-brand copy for this channel.

extensions.description: Brand-specific or vendor-specific fields not covered by this schema. Must use a namespaced key per §9.2.
